calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Set For Trial On Jul-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Michael Wright'S Demurrer To Complaint

Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Shoshana Raphael of the law offices of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC (email: Shoshana@zfplaw.com) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, John Paul D. Visaya of the law offices of Tenderloin Housing Clinic (email: johnv@thclinic.org) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Michael Wright's Demurrer To Complaint is OVERRULED. This is an ejectment action following termination of defendant's tenancy under the Ellis Act. Defendant cites no authority for the notion that he may dispute wording of Ellis Act notices at this late date, 17 months after they were served. In any event, fairly read, the notices were appropriate.

To defendant's first point, the cover letter quotes the exact relevant language of the rent ordinance. Moreover, the commonsense reading of "No party shall be relieved of the duty to perform any obligation under the lease or rental agreement during the extended tenancy" is the usual Ellis Act period plus the added time for defendant's disability, the whole being "the extended tenancy." Defendant's construction that a landlord need not comply with the rental agreement on days 1-120 but then must comply on days 121-365 is not a fair reading.

Second, the cover letter phrase "subject to Cal. Gov. Code sec. 7060.3" immediately follows "future owner" and sec. 7060.3 can only be logically read to apply to future owners, not to a "current owner."

Third, the cover letter states that a tenant has reoccupation rights "under Section 37.9A(c) of the Rent Ordinance." Defendant cites nothing suggesting that the rights must be described or attached - though the full text of Rent Ordinance sec. 37.9A was attached - and the rent ordinance is available in seconds on the internet.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Rihard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
